title: H-L1 macro-event proxy baseline
purpose: Pre-register a deterministic macro-event and volatility-regime proxy baseline before waiting on GDELT or running live LLM calls.
hypothesis_context: H-L1 asks whether LLM measurements of real pre-entry news add information beyond deterministic VIX and macro-calendar baselines. Since real timestamp-clean news cases are still blocked, this proxy baseline tests the deterministic part first and decides whether better news collection remains justified.
research_question: Do already-available timestamp-clean macro-calendar labels and VIX/VXV regimes explain adverse ORB/trade conditions enough to form a baseline that future LLM/news scores must beat?

macro_event_labels: no scheduled high-importance macro event day
volatility_labels: VIX/VIX3M term-structure risk label where available
baseline_definition.decision_timestamp_policy: Use only labels known before the strategy decision timestamp. Scheduled macro labels may use known calendar entries; VIX/VXV labels must use prior-close or otherwise pre-entry observable values.

planned_tests.question: Do scheduled macro-event days show different adverse-day or trade-outcome behavior from no-event days?
Do prior-close VIX/VXV regimes explain adverse ORB/trade conditions better than macro-event labels alone?
Does the combined deterministic baseline create a stronger comparison target for future LLM/news scores?
Is there enough deterministic signal or unresolved residual risk to justify continued timestamp-clean news collection?

split_policy.reference_pre_break_period: 2019-01-01 to 2022-05-10 if local aligned data exists
split_policy.train_or_design_period: 2022-05-11 to 2023-12-31 where local aligned data exists
split_policy.oos_period: 2024-01-01 to current available data where local aligned data exists

allowed_claims: The deterministic macro/VIX baseline is useful or weak as a comparison target for future LLM/news measurement.
Future LLM/news scores must add information beyond this baseline.
Better timestamp-clean news collection is or is not justified at E1 proxy level.
This is E1 proxy evidence only after the future run, not LLM/news evidence.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim an LLM gate was tested.
Do not claim real news sentiment was tested.
Do not claim timestamp-clean news cases exist.
Do not run OpenRouter or any live LLM call from this preregistration.
Do not run a GDELT live retry from this preregistration.
Do not use paid news providers from this preregistration.
Do not claim E2 acceptance-grade evidence.
future_run_completion_criteria: All planned deterministic baseline tests are run or explicitly marked unavailable with source evidence.
Every event, VIX, and combined bucket reports sample count.
Chronological split and no-OOS-tuning discipline are stated.
MinTRL/PSR/DSR, under-sampled, underpowered, and big-day dependency handling is reported where applicable.
The report clearly states that this is not LLM/news evidence.
The next action states whether timestamp-clean news collection remains justified before live LLM prompt research.
